english: sophy in India (Banerjea Dialogues on Hindoo Philosophy p. 56f); but in the Buddhist legends it is mentioned as the name of one of the ancestors of the Sakya-race, on which account Buddha is often called Gautama. The different forms of this name are used promiscuously by Tibetan writers.

ashes, *gog-tál yám-će* (gog thal yam ce), *yóg-će* (yog ce), *tiṅ-će* (ting ce) W. to spread ashes (viz. on the snow, in order to increase the effects of the sun, and to accelerate the thawing of the snow).
1. to crawl (of little children). 2. to crumble off, to scale off (of the plaster of a wall, of scurf etc.).

dilapidated, ruinous, *káṅ(-pa) góg(-po)* (khang (pa) gog (po)) a house in ruins; *mkár-góg* (mkhar gog) a dilapidated castle; of clothes: out of repair, ragged; *żiṅ-góg* (zhing gog) a field lying waste; *dpe-góg* (dpe gog) an antiquated, worthless book; *gog-góg* (gog gog) Cs.: 'the sound of a somewhat broken vessel'.

1. price, value, also *goṅ-táṅ* (gong thang) Glr., frq.; *goṅ dpyád-pa* (gong dpyad pa) (often also *bćád-pa* (bcad pa), inconsistent with etymology) to apprize, to fix a price; *goṅ brgyáb-pa* (gong brgyab pa) C. (*goṅ grig-pa* (gong grig pa) Schr., Sch.) id. — 2. the above, in space as well as in time, (in Khams e. g. it is used as a sbst., signifying: elevated, alpine pasture-grounds): the above said, the former, referring to a preceding part or passage of a book, *goṅ daṅ mtun* (gong dang mthun), *goṅ daṅ _drá-bar* (gong dang 'dra bar), *goṅ-bżin* (gong bzhin), *goṅ-mtsúṅs* (gong mtshungs) as above (mentioned); *priṅ goṅ _og _dzól-ba* (phrin gong 'og 'dzol ba) to confuse a message, to make a medley of it Glr.; *goṅ-du* (gong du), *goṅ-na* (gong na), *goṅ-nas* (gong nas), *goṅ-la* (gong la) 1. adv. over it, on it, thereon, above, from above. 2. postp. c. genit. or accus.: on, above, over, before, *sgo goṅ-du* (sgo gong du) over the door; *yáb-kyi goṅ-du _das* (yab kyi gong du 'das), he died before his father Glr.; *dėi goṅ-du* (de'i gong du) before this time Glr.; *ma tsogs goṅ-du* (ma tshogs gong du) before they are assembled; *goṅ-gi* (gong gi) the former, the above mentioned; *goṅ-gi dė-rnams* (gong gi de rnams) those preceding; *goṅ-du bsád-pa ltar* (gong du bshad pa ltar) as has been said, explained above; *goṅ-du smós-pa* (gong du smos pa) the above mentioned; *goṅ-gi ... żes smós-pa* (gong gi ... zhes smos pa) the above statement that ...; *sṅa goṅ bód-kyi rgyál-po* (snga gong bod kyi rgyal po) the former (ancient) Tibetan kings; *goṅ* (gong) and *_og* ('og) like our subdivisions of a and b, the first and second part, division or section of a book, *ba-goṅ* (ba gong) and *ba-_óg* (ba 'og) Volume XV Section 1 and 2; the face and the back of a leaf: *bżi-goṅ* (bzhi gong) folio 4, a. — *goṅ-sku-gżógs* (gong sku gzhogs), a title, like our: his highness, excellence, eminence Sch. v. sku.

W. *gog* (gog), lump, mass, heap, clot, *śá-goṅ-po* (sha gong po) a lump of flesh Dzl.; *krag-goṅ* (khrag gong) a clot of blood Glr.; *bol-goṅ* (bol gong) C., *sa-góg* (sa gog) W., clod, glebe; *ka-góg* (kha gog) W. snow-ball.
W. *goṅ-ṅa* (gong nga), *gós-kyi goṅ-ba* (gos kyi gong ba), collar, *goṅ-ba-nas _dzín-pa* (gong ba nas 'dzin pa) to seize by the collar.

a higher one, a superior; the former, the first named, *goṅ-ma bżin-du* (gong ma bzhin du) like the former; *rgyál-ba goṅ-ma* (rgyal ba gong ma) the most high, the divine Buddha Mil.; *goṅ-ma će* (gong ma che), *goṅ-ma ćen-po* (gong ma chen po) the most high, applied to worldly sovereigns, as: *rgya-nág goṅ-ma* (rgya nag gong ma) the emperor of China C.; *goṅ-ma-rnams* (gong ma rnams) Mil. the gods (the 'superi' of the ancients), among whom according to the doctrines of Buddhism the Lamas are included.

ptarmigan, white grouse, *lhá-bya goṅ-mo* (lha bya gong mo) B. and C., *ri-bya goṅ-mo* (ri bya gong mo) W., *goṅ-srég* (gong sreg) id. (?); *goṅ-yag* (gong yag) Sch.: wood-grouse, cock of the wood, Tetrao urogallus.

W. *gód-ka* (god ka) Dzl. *gód-pa* (god pa), Cs. *gód-ma* (god ma) 1. loss, damage, *god _gyúr-ba* (god 'gyur ba) Thgy., *gho'-la _dó-wa* (gho' la 'do wa) C., *god-la ćá-će* (god la cha ce) W., to suffer loss, e.g. *nór-la* (nor la) or *nor* (nor), a loss of money and property; *gód-pa* (god pa) vb. id., *nor gód-da* (nor god da) W. have you had a loss? 2. C. punishment.
I. vb. to put on (clothes, shoes), *mgó-la ża gón-pa* (mgo la zha gon pa) to put on a cap.
